reasoning: In late April 2025, a Politico article reported that the Trump administration was debating lifting sanctions on Russian energy assets, specifically mentioning Nord Stream 2. However, this report was met with an immediate, forceful, and joint public denial by the very officials named in the report (Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Special Envoy Steve Witkoff). They dismissed the report as "fictitious" and "unequivocally false," stating they had not engaged in such conversations. Given this strong public repudiation just a couple of months before the July 1, 2025 deadline, it is highly unlikely the administration will reverse course and officially delist NORD STREAM 2 AG from the OFAC SDN list within this short time frame. Furthermore, administrative actions such as removing an entity from the SDN list can take time even if a policy decision is made, and Congress could pose additional friction. Therefore, the probability is very low.
